user_prompt: when `Hotel` decides to shutdown, blank out the screen, draw `sprite_sleep_100` right in the center of the screen, wait 3 seconds, then shutdown. During this, `FlyGui` is not polling, nothing is polling or pumping.

the battery reading mechanism can issue a shutdown if the batter is too low, similarly blank out the screen, prevent any more polling from anything, draw `sprite_lowbatt_100` in the center of the screen
On boot, after `BattTracker::init();`, do one check of battery level to see if it needs to shutdown in the above way.
I would suggest the actual functionality of these be implemented in `lib\FlyGuiViews` under a file called `ShutdownView.cpp` but it doesn't have to be a class object, it's just like... 2 draw calls.
agent_reply: Added [ShutdownView.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ShutdownView.cpp:70) / [ShutdownView.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ShutdownView.h:6) to blank the display, center either `sprite_sleep_100` or `sprite_lowbatt_100`, wait 3 seconds, then power off/deep sleep. It sets a shared shutdown-in-progress flag first.

Routed Hotel shutdown through the sleep icon path in [Hotel.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/Hotel/Hotel.cpp:268). Added BattTracker low-battery shutdown checks in [BattTracker.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BattTracker/BattTracker.cpp:137), with shutdown triggered when the tracked level is low and not charging. Added the boot check right after `BattTracker::init();` and loop guards in [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:310), so both cores stop GUI/audio/Wi-Fi/Bluetooth polling once shutdown begins.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build succeeded. `src/version.c` still has its pre-existing generated dirty diff; I left it alone.
